context:
## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Ship `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` and `docs/PROVIDERS.md` as the single, cohesive, non-duplicated cross-cutting reference surface for stagehand v1.0, such that every value documented (FR34 precedence, 6 `STAGEHAND_*` env vars, `stagehand.*` git-config keys, `.stagehand.toml` schema, defaults table, the 10 CLI flags + exit codes, the six built-in providers, the manifest schema, field-merge rule, user-provider recipe, and the §12.7.1 tools-disable asymmetry) is accurate against the shipped binary, mutually consistent across the two files, and linked from the README.

**Deliverable**: Two finalized markdown files — `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` and `docs/PROVIDERS.md` — at `/home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/docs/`. Both already exist as accurate Mode A slices; this task (a) ADDS the missing §12.7.1 tools-disable asymmetry section to PROVIDERS.md, (b) de-dups and keeps the two files mutually consistent (especially the field-merge rule present in both), (c) confirms README links both docs and the blurbs match, and (d) final-proofs every value against PRD §15/§16/§12 and the live binary. No new code; no behavioral change.

**Success Definition**: `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` remain green (docs edits broke nothing); `./stagehand providers list` / `providers show pi` output matches the documented format byte-for-byte in substance; PROVIDERS.md contains an honest §12.7.1 split (pi/claude use explicit tool-disable flags; codex/cursor/gemini use read-only constraints); CONFIGURATION.md and PROVIDERS.md agree verbatim on the field-merge rule with no contradiction; README links both docs; a diff-vs-PRD proofread finds zero stale values (defaults, env var names, git-config keys, flag set, exit codes, six built-in manifest values).

## Current state (verified during research — do NOT re-derive)

Both target files ALREADY EXIST and are accurate Mode A slices:
- `docs/CONFIGURATION.md` (17 KB) — owns the config reference: §1 FR34 precedence (7 levels), §2 the 6 `STAGEHAND_*` env vars, §3 `stagehand.*` git-config keys (+`--bool`), §4 `.stagehand.toml` `[defaults]/[generation]/[provider.<name>]`, §5 field-merge, §6 §19 trust notice, §7 `config init/path`, §8 CLI flag table (§15.2), §9 exit codes (§15.4).
- `docs/PROVIDERS.md` (8.5 KB) — owns the providers reference: `providers list` output format, `providers show <name>` TOML output + §12.1 manifest-schema key table, the field-merge note (FR48), the six built-ins table, a Roadmap note about `providers/*.toml`.

The Mode A slices were written by P1.M5.T3.S1 (config), P1.M7.T2.S1 (CLI flags/env + exit codes), P1.M7.T3.S1 (providers list/show — folding the conceptual P1.M2.T3.S1 slice). The install docstring lives in the `install.sh` head comment (distribution tasks).

## CONFIRMED GAP (the one real piece of work)

`docs/PROVIDERS.md` is MISSING the **PRD §12.7.1 "tools-disable asymmetry"** section that this task's contract explicitly requires. Verified: `grep -ci 'asymmetr\|read-only' docs/PROVIDERS.md` → 0. The six built-ins currently appear only as a one-line table; the honest architectural split behind their differing `bare_flags` idioms is undocumented there.

The canonical source is **PRD.md §12.7.1 (line 716)**. Adapt it (do not copy verbatim — translate to the docs/ voice) into a new PROVIDERS.md section, e.g. `## How the six built-ins become bare (§12.7.1 tools-disable asymmetry)`, covering:
- **Explicit tool-disable flags** (a literal off switch → pure text-in/text-out, no agent loop): `pi` (`--no-tools`), `claude` (`--tools ""`).
- **Read-only constraint instead** (no global tool-off switch; tools are intrinsic, so we constrain to read-only + never-ask): `codex` (`--sandbox read-only`), `cursor` (`--mode ask`), `gemini` (`--approval-mode default`).
- The three stated consequences (safety preserved either way — repo-integrity invariant §18.1 holds for all six; latency may vary on the read-only-constrained agents; output is still just the final assistant message regardless of internal tool reasoning).
- A pointer that the `bare_flags` field exists precisely so each provider expresses "bare" in its own idiom (this is design, not a defect — PRD §12.7.1's framing).

Cross-reference the concrete `bare_flags` values the user can see via `stagehand providers show <name>` (e.g. `--no-tools` for pi, `--sandbox read-only` + `--ephemeral` for codex, `--mode ask --trust` for cursor).

## Cross-doc duplication analysis (keep consistent, do NOT delete)

The provider **field-merge rule** intentionally appears in BOTH files — they serve different readers and must be kept in agreement:
- `docs/CONFIGURATION.md §5` — the config-syntax + precedence lens (how `[provider.<name>]` overrides layer per-key shallow, then field-merge once over the built-in inside `NewRegistry`).
- `docs/PROVIDERS.md` "Field-merge: overriding a provider (FR48)" — the show-output lens (what `providers show <name>` reveals after the merge).

Both currently agree verbatim on the rule: field-by-field merge; only set fields override; slices/maps (`subcommand`/`bare_flags`/`env`) replaced WHOLESALE not deep-merged; an override naming an unknown provider is added as a brand-new provider used as-is. The consolidation must VERIFY they still agree word-for-word on these four points and make the cross-link explicit (PROVIDERS.md already says "See CONFIGURATION.md §5"). Do NOT delete either copy — each is the right doc for its audience.

The §12.8 user-provider TOML example is currently IDENTICAL across README ↔ CONFIGURATION.md §4 golden ↔ PRD §12.8/§16.2 (`[provider.myagent] ... bare_flags = ["--no-mcp", "--ephemeral"]`). PROVIDERS.md references §12.8 only in a single sentence in the field-merge section — add one clearer pointer ("to define a brand-new agent see CONFIGURATION.md §4 / PRD §12.8") so a reader landing on PROVIDERS.md is not stranded.

## Documentation & References

```yaml
  critical: §12.7.1 (line 716) is the canonical text for the missing PROVIDERS.md section — adapt its three-consequence framing. §15.2 lists exactly 10 flags (no manual --version/--help — cobra built-ins). §15.4 lists exit 0/1/2/3 + 124 reserved.
- file: internal/provider/builtin.go
  why: The truth behind every provider table cell — the six real manifests with their verified bare_flags idioms.
  pattern: pi(--no-tools), claude(--tools "" + --setting-sources "" + --tools "" + --disable-slash-commands + --no-chrome + --no-session-persistence), gemini(--approval-mode default), opencode(none), codex(--sandbox read-only + --ephemeral), cursor(--mode ask --trust).
  gotcha: codex uses prompt_delivery=stdin (NOT positional) and --ephemeral (§C correction #2); codex bare_flags intentionally LACK --ask-for-approval (the real-agent suite P1.M8.T3.S1 proved `codex exec` rejects it on codex-cli 0.142.4) — do NOT let the asymmetry section imply codex uses --ask-for-approval.

  why: The truth behind §5 field-merge + §6 §19 trust notice (string 'stagehand: repo-local config changed provider to <name>', repo-local-only trigger).

  why: EDIT target (final-proof + keep consistent with PROVIDERS.md). Already accurate.

- docfile: plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/external_deps.md
  why: Provenance of the six verified manifests (§B) + the four §C corrections — the why behind each bare_flags idiom, in case a value is questioned during proofread.
  section: §B (verified manifests), §C (corrections)
```

## Proofread checklist (the core of consolidation)

After the asymmetry gap is filled, walk this list against the live binary + PRD and fix any drift:
1. Defaults table (CONFIGURATION.md §1) — 8 rows, values match `internal/config/defaults.go`.
2. Env vars (§2) — exactly SIX `STAGEHAND_*` (PROVIDER, MODEL, TIMEOUT, CONFIG, VERBOSE, NO_COLOR); note underscore in `STAGEHAND_NO_COLOR`; auto_stage_all + generation scalars have NO env/flag.
3. Git-config keys (§3) — camelCase; `--bool` for the booleans; no `[provider.*]` key (git-config cannot express tables).
4. TOML tables (§4) — `[defaults]`, `[generation]`, `[provider.<name>]` field sets match the loader DTOs.
5. Field-merge (CONFIGURATION.md §5 ↔ PROVIDERS.md) — identical 4-point rule; cross-link present.
6. Trust notice (§6) — exact §19 string; repo-local-only trigger.
7. CLI flags (§8) — 10 flags, shorthands `-a`/`-v`, `--version` via cobra field, `--timeout 0` = use-config-default note, verbose v1-gap note.
8. Exit codes (§9) — 0/1/2/3 + 124 reserved (timeout collapses to ErrRescue→3).
9. Six built-ins (PROVIDERS.md table) — command, delivery, default_model match builtin.go.
10. Manifest schema (PROVIDERS.md TOML-key table) — maps 1:1 to the 18 `provider.Manifest` fields.
11. README blurbs — match the finalized doc content.

## Known Gotchas

- This is Mode B CONSOLIDATION, not greenfield. Both files exist and are accurate — resist rewriting wholesale; make surgical, behavior-accurate edits. Wholesale rewrites risk introducing drift the proofread is meant to catch.
- Do NOT let the new §12.7.1 section imply codex uses `--ask-for-approval` — the real-agent suite (P1.M8.T3.S1, 2026-07-03) proved `codex exec` rejects that flag; codex bare_flags are `--sandbox read-only` + `--ephemeral` only.
- Keep the two field-merge copies consistent — if you tighten wording in one, mirror it in the other so they do not drift into contradiction.
- README is owned by sibling P1.M8.T4.S1 — verify linkage + blurb accuracy only; do not restructure it.
- No markdown linter is configured (no markdownlint/mdl/.prettierrc; `.editorconfig` has no max_line_length). Validation is file-existence + grep content checks + binary smoke tests + go build/test (regression guard). Do not invent a linter gate.

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- ❌ Don't duplicate full content that already lives once in the right doc (e.g. don't copy the whole golden `.stagehand.toml` into PROVIDERS.md — cross-link it).
- ❌ Don't soften or omit the §12.7.1 honest framing — the asymmetry is documented "honestly" per PRD; hiding it defeats the section's purpose.
- ❌ Don't fabricate provider values — every cell in every table must be verifiable via `./stagehand providers show <name>` or builtin.go.
- ❌ Don't touch any .go file or break the import/build graph — this is a docs-only task; `go build ./...` must stay green.
- ❌ Don't delete either field-merge copy; keep them consistent instead.
- ❌ Don't edit README structure (owned by P1.M8.T4.S1) — verify links/blurb only.
